title: Taxable income or loss 2024
description: Complete this question to declare your total taxable income or loss.
keywords: Individual tax return instructions, income, taxable income, loss, taxable loss, instruction, tax return, 2024, paper form, paper return, paper lodgement

content_blocks.text: Things you need to know
Declare at this question your total taxable income or loss.
How you complete it will depend on whether you completed question L1 Tax losses of earlier income years – labels F or Z in your tax return.

If the amount at Taxable income or loss in your tax return is a loss (if you printed L in the Loss box), then this may not be the amount you show as losses carried forward from earlier income years at question L1 in next year's tax return. Adjustments will have to be made to take into account any exempt income, deductions for gifts or donations (question D9 ) or personal superannuation contributions (question D12 in your supplementary tax return). For more information, see Amounts you do not include as income or contact us .
Have you completed question L1 – labels F or Z ?

items: No – Go to No prior year losses .
Yes – Go to Deducting your prior year losses .

content_blocks.text: Completing your tax return
To complete this question, use the information below.
No prior year losses
Follow the step below.
Step 1
Transfer the amount you have shown at Subtotal on page 4 in your tax return to Taxable income or loss .
If the amount at Taxable income or loss is less than zero, write L in the Loss box. Keep a record of this amount to work out your tax losses of earlier income years for next year.
You have now completed this section, see Where to go next .
Deducting your prior year losses
Follow the step below.
Step 2
Add up the amounts at question L1 – labels F and Z and subtract the total from the amount you have written at Subtotal .
Step 3
Write the answer at Taxable income or loss in your tax return.

You cannot have a loss at Taxable income or loss if you had amounts at labels F or Z . This is because you can only use your losses from earlier years to reduce your current year taxable income to nil. You cannot use them to create a tax loss. Any losses you have remaining can be carried over to future years until they have been fully recouped.
Where to go next

items: Go to question T1 Seniors and pensioners tax offset 2024 .
Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2024 .
Go back to question L1 Tax losses of earlier income years 2024 .

content_text:
## Things you need to know
Declare at this question your total taxable income or loss.
How you complete it will depend on whether you completed question L1 Tax losses of earlier income years – labels F or Z in your tax return.
If the amount at Taxable income or loss in your tax return is a loss (if you printed L in the Loss box), then this may not be the amount you show as losses carried forward from earlier income years at question L1 in next year's tax return. Adjustments will have to be made to take into account any exempt income, deductions for gifts or donations (question D9 ) or personal superannuation contributions (question D12 in your supplementary tax return). For more information, see Amounts you do not include as income or contact us .
Have you completed question L1 – labels F or Z ?
- No – Go to No prior year losses .
- Yes – Go to Deducting your prior year losses .
## Completing your tax return
To complete this question, use the information below.
### No prior year losses
Follow the step below.
#### Step 1
Transfer the amount you have shown at Subtotal on page 4 in your tax return to Taxable income or loss .
If the amount at Taxable income or loss is less than zero, write L in the Loss box. Keep a record of this amount to work out your tax losses of earlier income years for next year.
You have now completed this section, see Where to go next .
### Deducting your prior year losses
Follow the step below.
#### Step 2
Add up the amounts at question L1 – labels F and Z and subtract the total from the amount you have written at Subtotal .
#### Step 3
Write the answer at Taxable income or loss in your tax return.
You cannot have a loss at Taxable income or loss if you had amounts at labels F or Z . This is because you can only use your losses from earlier years to reduce your current year taxable income to nil. You cannot use them to create a tax loss. Any losses you have remaining can be carried over to future years until they have been fully recouped.
## Where to go next
- Go to question T1 Seniors and pensioners tax offset 2024 .
- Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2024 .
- Go back to question L1 Tax losses of earlier income years 2024 .
